# eval.gnubg_harness { #raccoon.eval.gnubg_harness }

`eval.gnubg_harness`

Automated money-game sessions against GNUBG via the `gnubg-nn` package.

This is the M6 benchmark harness. Raccoon (driven by MCTS on a RaccoonNet)
plays a cubeless money game against GNUBG's neural-net evaluator. Gammons
and backgammons are scored (2x / 3x) via OpenSpiel's terminal_result.

The GNUBG FFI is isolated in ``raccoon.eval.gnubg_adapter`` so this module
stays free of conditional imports.

## Classes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [GnubgHarness](#raccoon.eval.gnubg_harness.GnubgHarness) | Play a session of money games between Raccoon and GNUBG. |

### GnubgHarness { #raccoon.eval.gnubg_harness.GnubgHarness }

```python
eval.gnubg_harness.GnubgHarness(
    raccoon_network,
    gnubg_level='world',
    num_simulations=200,
    ply=None,
    log_games=False,
    raccoon_version='unknown',
)
```

Play a session of money games between Raccoon and GNUBG.

``gnubg_level`` is a friendly string (e.g. ``"world"``) mapped to a ply
integer via ``gnubg_adapter.level_to_ply``. Pass ``ply`` explicitly to
override (``0`` = fastest, ``2`` = strongest).

#### Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [play_match](#raccoon.eval.gnubg_harness.GnubgHarness.play_match) | Play ``num_games`` money games and return aggregated results. |

##### play_match { #raccoon.eval.gnubg_harness.GnubgHarness.play_match }

```python
eval.gnubg_harness.GnubgHarness.play_match(num_games=1000)
```

Play ``num_games`` money games and return aggregated results.
